name: Cherry-Pistachio Biscotti
recipeIngredient: 1/4 stick cold butter; cut into small pieces
3 large Eggs; beaten with a fork
1 1/4 cups Pistachio nuts; shelled, lightly salted
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Lightly grease a large baking sheet.
3. In a food processor, combine flour, sugar, lime peel, and baking powder until blended.
4. Add butter and cherries; pulse until the cherries are coarsely chopped.
5. Spoon off about 1 tablespoon of beaten eggs and set aside.
6. Add remaining eggs and pistachios to the food processor; pulse until the dough is evenly moistened.
7. Turn the dough out onto a well-floured work surface. Divide into four equal portions.
8. Roll each portion into a 9-inch log.
9. Place logs across the prepared baking sheet, about 3 inches apart.
10. Press down on the logs to flatten them to about 2 inches wide.
11. Brush the logs with the reserved beaten eggs.
12. Bake for 25 minutes or until golden brown.
13. Let cool on the sheet for 10 minutes on a wire rack.
14. Loosen the biscuits and transfer to a cutting board; let cool for another 10 minutes.
15. Using a large, sharp knife, cut each log diagonally into 1/2-inch-thick slices.
16. Arrange the slices upright on a clean baking sheet (no need to grease again).
17. Bake for an additional 14-15 minutes until crisp.
18. Remove from the oven and let cool completely on a wire rack.
